title: silene pusilla subsp. candavica
intro: Silene pusilla subsp. candavica, also known as the small-flowered catchfly, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the caryophyllaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and improving digestion. In herbal preparations, Silene pusilla subsp. candavica is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
